free_text: Helium ion currents of 25 microA for 1 hr were used
at full cyclotron energy, but at reduced helium ion
energies a current of 15 microA was required to
two opposing 2pi gas flow Geiger tubes. The
efficiency of these counters was approximately 70 per
cent for a 1-MeV beta emitter and the backgrounds
were on the order of 2.0 +- 0.1 counts per minute.
Targets of each of the three enriched
platinum isotopes were bombarded by 41 and 34 MeV
helium ions. After bombardment, the aluminum foil
containing the recoil fission products was dissolved
in a solution of HC1-HNO3 and carriers of the
isotopes to be separated. The radiochemical
